Class Deck

java.lang.Object
com.example.elcincuentazo.model.Deck
public class Deck extends Object
Manages the draw pile (mazo) and the central table pile (mesa) for a Cincuentazo game.

On construction a full 52-card deck is created (values 1–13 for each of the 4 suits) and shuffled. Cards are drawn from the top of the deck. When the deck is exhausted, the table pile (excluding the top card, which remains as the last played card) is reshuffled and becomes the new deck.

The table pile is stored as a Deque to allow O(1) access to both the top card and the full history.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Deck()
    Constructs and fully initialises a new Deck.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Card
    drawCard()
    Draws and returns the top card from the draw pile.
    int
    drawPileSize()
    Returns the number of cards remaining in the draw pile.
    List<Card>
    getTablePile()
    Returns an unmodifiable snapshot of the entire table pile in play order (index 0 = most recently played).
    boolean
    isDrawPileEmpty()
    Returns true if the draw pile is empty.
    Card
    peekTableTop()
    Returns the card currently on top of the table pile (the last one played), without removing it.
    void
    placeOnTable(Card card)
    Places a card onto the table pile (marks it as location 2).
    void
    returnCardsToBottom(List<Card> cards)
    Places all cards from eliminated players back at the bottom of the draw pile, as specified by the game rules.
    int
    tablePileSize()
    Returns the number of cards currently on the table pile.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Deck

      public Deck()
      Constructs and fully initialises a new Deck. Creates 52 cards (13 values × 4 suits), shuffles them, and places them all in the draw pile.

  • Method Details

    • drawCard

      public Card drawCard()
      Draws and returns the top card from the draw pile.

      If the draw pile is empty this method automatically refills it from the table pile (all cards except the current top card) and reshuffles, mirroring the official rule. If refilling is also impossible (table has only one card or no cards), an EmptyDeckException is thrown.

      Returns:
      the top Card from the draw pile (location set to 0)
      Throws:
      EmptyDeckException - if the draw pile and table pile are both exhausted

    • returnCardsToBottom

      public void returnCardsToBottom(List<Card> cards)
      Places all cards from eliminated players back at the bottom of the draw pile, as specified by the game rules.
      Parameters:
      cards - the list of cards to return to the bottom of the deck

    • placeOnTable

      public void placeOnTable(Card card)
      Places a card onto the table pile (marks it as location 2).
      Parameters:
      card - the card being placed on the table; must not be null

    • peekTableTop

      public Card peekTableTop()
      Returns the card currently on top of the table pile (the last one played), without removing it.
      Returns:
      top card of the table pile, or null if the table is empty

    • getTablePile

      public List<Card> getTablePile()
      Returns an unmodifiable snapshot of the entire table pile in play order (index 0 = most recently played).
      Returns:
      list of all table cards

    • drawPileSize

      public int drawPileSize()
      Returns the number of cards remaining in the draw pile.
      Returns:
      draw-pile size

    • tablePileSize

      public int tablePileSize()
      Returns the number of cards currently on the table pile.
      Returns:
      table-pile size

    • isDrawPileEmpty

      public boolean isDrawPileEmpty()
      Returns true if the draw pile is empty.
      Returns:
      true when no cards are left to draw
